title: Cassus Fett
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}}
{{Character
|type=Mandalorian

{{Quote|You listen to Cassus Fett, and your son's sons will have nothing to do—because you'll have conquered everything!|Cassus Fett to [[Gormer]]|<ref name="KOTOR 23" />}}
'''Cassus Fett''' was a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Field Marshal/Legends|Field Marshal]] who lived during the time of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Fett was an aide-de-camp to [[Mandalore the Ultimate|Mand'alor the Ultimate]], leader of the Mandalorians, and he was highly influential in areas throughout Mandalorian society.

Fett spearheaded the promotion of the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusader]] movement, a sect of Mandalorian society with a more regimented structure and [[Neo-Crusader armor|standardized armor]] than the hitherto dominant [[Mandalorian Crusaders]]. By exerting control over the clans and providing a means of converting conquered cultures into Neo-Crusaders, Fett turned the Mandalorians into a war machine intent on [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conquest.

On the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], Fett engaged the forces of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in a series of battles, including a [[Battle of Cathar|massacre]] of the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] leading to their near annihilation as a species on the planet [[Cathar (location)/Legends|Cathar]]. He engineered a number of minor engagements at the edge of Republic space to determine the strength of the Republic, before launching into a full-scale invasion of Republic space along three fronts in [[3963 BBY]].

Fett was present at the preliminary [[Battle of Vanquo]], led the [[Taris Siege|siege of Taris]] and eventual conquest of that world, planned an invasion of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], and killed the [[Unidentified Republic captain (Cassus Fett)|captain of a Republic flagship]] at the [[Battle of Jaga's Cluster]]. After becoming the most wanted man in the galaxy, Fett was presumed dead by the time of the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Many years later, two other members of his Clan, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango]] and [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], would live up to the clan set's legacy with their skill.

==Biography==
===Rise to power===
The origins of Cassus Fett were largely unknown in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]; [[Republic Intelligence/Legends|Republic Intelligence]] was unaware of where he came from, and even few of his fellow [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] knew, though it was known that he was [[Human/Legends|Human]]. After the close of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] and the defeat of the Mandalorians in it, a new [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] rose to power, styled [[Mandalore the Ultimate]]. Mandalore, looking to redefine Mandalorian identity, called together a number of talented advisors with abilities beyond battle and war, amongst them Fett. Fett became Mandalore's aide-de-camp<ref name="KOTORCG" /> and top strategist;<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> he was highly influential, both on the battlefield and off. He helped convert the disorganized rabble of the Mandalorians into an efficient, regimented organization.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

[[File:Poscassus.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Cassus Fett and Mandalore the Ultimate.]]
Whereas the Mandalorians before Fett's time had simply conquered worlds without bringing them into the fold of Mandalorian culture, Fett saw a need to use their conquests to their advantage. He and Mandalore the Ultimate took notice of a cult that had sprung up amongst the Mandalorians. Known as the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Neo-Crusaders]], this group lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]]; they broke from Mandalorian tradition by wearing identical suits of armor and had an unorthodox chain of command and system of labor division. Fett convinced Mandalore to incorporate their ideals, and the two Mandalorians encouraged this movement. Though Mandalore decreed the Neo-Crusader system to have widespread usage, it was Fett who implemented it. Fett converted the small-time organizational system of the Neo-Crusader cults used into one that could be used on a large scale, and he incorporated Neo-Crusaders amongst the clans to spread their ideals throughout.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Fett believed that similar uniforms could help exert dominion over the Mandalorians, who were largely growing out of control.<ref name="KOTOR 20">[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]]</ref> He also argued that the suits of armor the Neo-Crusaders wore should be color-coded by rank, so that new recruits could see where authority lay. While the lowest ranks wore blue armor, Mandalore's top advisors wore gold-colored suits, and Fett was one of the first to don the armor as a sign that all Mandalorians, regardless of status, should forgo their individualism. Before the invasion of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] began, Fett's system for converting conquered cultures into new Mandalorians was in place.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

Fett was not without opposition amongst the Mandalorians, though, considering Mandalorians like [[Demagol]] and [[Pulsipher]],<ref name="KOTORCG" /> a cohort of Demagol's,<ref name="KOTOR 10">[[Knights of the Old Republic 10|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 10]]</ref> as his rivals. Demagol, a Mandalorian scientist, was another of those who Mandalore included in his circle of advisors, and Fett believed him to be a rival for Mandalore's attention. Demagol irked Fett in return by refusing to wear Neo-Crusader armor.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

===The beginnings of war===
{{Quote|The Cathar left a stain of dishonor on the ''Mando'ade''. Today, I wash it clean in the waters of their own presumption.|Cassus Fett executes the Cathar species|<ref name="KOTOR42">[[Knights of the Old Republic 42|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 42]]</ref>}}
In the years surrounding the beginning of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], but before the Mandalorian invasion of the Republic, the Mandalorians were an active force in the worlds of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. Fett conquered a number of unaligned worlds in this time, earning him a reputation as a master of military command. In [[3976 BBY]], Fett led an [[Battle of Cathar|attack]] on the planet [[Cathar (location)/Legends|Cathar]] that devastated that world,<ref name="KOTORCG" /> seeking to punish the native [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] for opposing the Mandalorians in struggles long past. Leading an army of warriors on [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]], Fett drove the Cathar into near-extinction.<ref name="NEC" /> The remaining Cathar, defeated, were rounded up and herded into the planet's sea. As Fett prepared to execute them, a [[Unidentified Mandalorian female|lone Mandalorian]] stood with the Cathar, pleading with Fett to spare them. Fett stood his ground, and his ships poured their fire onto the Cathar and the lone warrior.<ref name="KOTOR42" /> The massacre was so great that those Cathar who remained were afraid to speak of it for years. The Republic covered it up, fearing that knowledge of the attack would destabilize reconstruction after the Sith War.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> The disappearance of the Cathar remained a mystery for over a dozen years.<ref name="KOTOR42" />

[[File:CassusF.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Cassus Fett in combat with a [[Municipal patrol droid Mark I]].]]
Shortly before the invasion of the Republic proper, Fett helped to engineer the [[Mandalorian Wars#False War|False War]], a brief engagement in the Outer Rim with the forces of the Republic. It consisted of feigning weakness until the strength of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]], revitalized after the Sith War, could be determined. Fett formulated the next part of the Mandalorian military plan as well: a three-front invasion of the Republic known as the Onslaught. This assault, launched in [[3964 BBY]], took advantage of the recent confusion the Republic had been thrown into, with the [[Padawan Massacre|murder]] of four [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] and the escape of [[Zayne Carrick]], the Jedi falsely accused of the crime.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> The planet [[Vanquo]] was one of the Mandalorians' targets, intended to draw off Republic forces to pave the way for an assault on the important world of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. Fett and Mandalore monitored the invasion from Mandalore's flagship, watching the progress of the Vanquo assault while they prepared to move on Taris. Fett had an orbital screen established around Vanquo so that the main force of the assault could engage the major settlements on the world's day side, while shock troopers landed on the night side to take out the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] camps there.<ref name="KOTOR8">[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]]</ref> Before the Republic could mount a defense, Fett and the Mandalorians had seized a number of worlds.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

As he had done with Mandalorian organization, Fett also overhauled Mandalorian military production. He set up [[Neo-Crusader War Forge|War Forges]] near the staging grounds of the Neo-Crusaders on [[Jebble]], [[Tarnith]], and other worlds. Quickly coming online, they provided the Mandalorian advance with the armor and weapons needed by new recruits.<ref name="KOTORCG" />

===The invasion of Taris===
{{Quote|This day, I release you from all allegiance to the Galactic Republic. The ''Mando'ade'' are your clan now.|Cassus Fett, to the people of Taris|<ref name="Taris Holofeed Special Proclamation">{{KOTORbackups|21}}</ref>}}
By [[3963 BBY]], the [[Taris Siege|attack]] on Taris had begun in earnest. When the Republic's defensive [[Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line]] fell to the Mandalorians, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Jimas Veltraa]] reformed his forces around Taris. They were ultimately destroyed, however, and the Mandalorians laid siege to the planet.<ref name="TAL">{{KOTORbackups|13}}</ref> As Mandalorian vessels moved into orbit around Taris with more arriving continually, adding their own firepower to the planetary bombardment, repeated attempts by Tarisian planetary defense fighters to break the siege were unsuccessful.<ref name="Taris Siege">{{KOTORbackups|15}}</ref> The Mandalorians were busy on other fronts, as well. They [[Battle of Serroco|laid waste]] to the world of [[Serroco]] in an attack orchestrated by Fett. When Demagol was reported dead at [[Flashpoint Stellar Research Station]]—actually having been captured by the Republic—Fett's influence rose.<ref name="Handbook">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]''</ref> Mandalore personally sent Fett to Taris to aid in the siege and help with the logistics of a planetary conquest of such magnitude. The experience would help them in their eventual invasion of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],<ref name="KOTOR 22">[[Knights of the Old Republic 22|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 22]]</ref> which Fett intended to be part of.<ref name="KOTOR 23" />

Eventually the time came to take the planet. The Mandalorians began landing their soldiers in Taris' polar regions, following this with landings in the equatorial regions, and eventually leading to a planet-wide invasion. With what a Tarisian reporter called "macabre efficiency," Fett led his troops, on foot or Basilisk war droid, to overtake the [[Upper City]]. [[Swoop gang/Legends|Swoop gangs]] briefly took shots at the Mandalorians before retreating into hiding, and soon even the news organs were interrupted as the conquest swept the planet.<ref name="Taris Invasion">{{KOTORbackups|18}}</ref> With the Mandalorians' victory over the planet secure, Fett made an announcement across the world on the [[Taris Holofeed]]. Telling the people of Taris that the Republic had failed them, Fett freed them from their allegiance to the Republic and [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]].<ref name="Taris Special">{{KOTORbackups|21}}</ref> As he had done on a number of other worlds, Fett gave the citizens of Taris a choice between joining the Neo-Crusaders or working in their factories as ''de facto'' [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> With a final promise of death to those who would betray the Mandalorians, Fett deactivated the Holofeed.<ref name="Taris Special" />

[[File:Cassus_Tower.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Fett monitors the Taris invasion from the Jedi Tower.]]
As the siege of Taris continued, Fett saw the tangle that arose among vessels of the invasion force, appropriated vehicles, and ships of attempted escapees.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> His own forces found themselves in each others' way, sometimes causing the loss of important materiél.<ref name="KOTOR 23" /> It could have slowed the Mandalorians for months, but from his headquarters in the former [[Jedi Tower]] Fett managed traffic control on and around the world. Even as he did so, he continued to successfully wage war on the [[Taris Resistance]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> As the [[Lower City]] of Taris was still being conquered, Fett ordered the city's skylanes to be kept clear for landing ships,<ref name="KOTOR 22" /> and those above the planet he personally directed to their destinations.<ref name="KOTOR 23" />

When news that Fett was headquartered in the Tower reached the Resistance, they decided to assassinate him. Their plan was to detonate a number of demolition charges in the foundations of the building, but to make sure that Fett was there when they attacked, they intended to infiltrate a reconnaissance into the Tower first.<ref name="KOTOR 23" /> Fett, meanwhile, studied the Resistance strong points in the Lower City and realized where they were hiding. He pulled his men out of the Tower and set off to attack the Resistance base, leaving the Resistance infiltration team unable to complete their mission. They destroyed the Tower anyway, but the rest of the Resistance forces were driven into retreat,<ref name="KOTOR 24">[[Knights of the Old Republic 24|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 24]]</ref> forced to fall back into the Taris [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]].<ref name="KOTOR 25">[[Knights of the Old Republic 25|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 25]]</ref>

Shortly after the invasion of Taris, the Republic began broadcasting propaganda on Mandalorian frequencies in an attempt to reduce morale and sway Neo-Crusader converts. Fett contacted Mandalorian officer [[Koblus Sornell]] to discuss the transmissions and any potential information in them. As the Mandalorians prepared an invasion of the planet [[Zongorlu]] from Taris, the Republic's broadcasts attempted to discourage them with reports of deadly conditions and dangerous inhabitants on the world, as well as immense ships at the Republic's command. Fett, meanwhile, had Sornell work on creating a mobile signal station for the invasion fleet, to communicate with him upon arrival. Sornell informed Fett of the problems that the transmissions were causing within the newly recruited ranks; warriors distracted by them had had several accidents, some of them deadly. When Sornell and the fleet at last invaded Zongorlu, they discovered that the broadcasts had been a farce; the world was temperate, its native species was a sessile plant, the massive ships did not exist, and the broadcasts had come from a tiny transmission station on the world. Sornell reported back to Fett with her findings, and Fett ordered her to hold station for a week, a much shorter time than the intended operation. While the transmission had ceased upon the invasion, they soon resumed, this time offering the Mandalorians a deal; in exchange for peace, the Republic would give them a portion of taxation from the worlds they had invaded. Sornell answered them with a refusal, and Fett soon contracted her, reprimanding her for not waiting. Sornell defended her decision.<ref name="Interference">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/interference20090929.html|text="Interference"|int=Interference|newhost=Suvudu}}</ref>

===The Jebble plague===
{{Quote|To stop the infection, Cassus amputated the limb. Very like him.|Demagol|<ref name="KOTOR 28">[[Knights of the Old Republic 28|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 28]]</ref>}}
After the battle with the Resistance, Fett took a number of troop transports and moved on to Jebble, a Mandalorian-controlled ice world being used as a staging area, where captured criminals were being trained into the Neo-Crusaders' ranks. With these new soldiers he planned to launch an invasion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], deep in Republic space. Before Fett could arrive, though, Pulsipher—<ref name="KOTOR 26">[[Knights of the Old Republic 26|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 26]]</ref> another Mandalorian rival of his—<ref name="KOTORCG" />reached Jebble on his own with a prize he had found in the Undercity of Taris: the [[Muur Talisman]], a powerful [[The Force/Legends|Force]] artifact. When he put it on, Pulsipher accidentally let loose a [[Rakghoul plague/Legends|plague]] that turned its victims into [[Rakghoul]]s. Soon, the ranks of Mandalorians stationed on Jebble were transforming into the beasts.<ref name="KOTOR 26" />

[[File:Cassus_Zayne.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Fett is contacted by Zayne Carrick.]]
Meanwhile, the Jedi fugitive Zayne Carrick had stowed away onto Pulsipher's transport and was now on Jebble. Having escaped the waves of Mandalorians and Rakghouls, Carrick found a communications station and contacted the approaching Mandalorian fleet. He warned Fett that the plague had broken out and told him not to land, but Fett, who knew that Carrick had been involved in the assassination attempt, was dubious. Meanwhile, Pulsipher, through use of the Talisman, had found himself in control of the Rakghouls; he intended to put Fett in his place by attacking the landing transports and, from there, spreading his influence throughout the galaxy. Pulsipher was killed, however,<ref name="KOTOR 27">[[Knights of the Old Republic 27|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 27]]</ref> and Carrick made his own escape from the planet when his friends came to his rescue. Fett arrived as Carrick was leaving, and, convinced of the Jedi's sincerity, annihilated the surface of the planet with missiles. He then contacted Carrick and stated he was in the Jedi's debt.<ref name="KOTOR 28" />

===All-out war===
Later that year, the truth about Cathar broke. A charismatic Jedi known as [[Revan/Legends|The Revanchist]] brought the Jedi to Cathar in search of answers. Upon finding the mask of the lone Mandalorian who had stood against Cassus Fett, the gathered Jedi had a vision of Fett's massacre. Spurred by the fate of the Cathar, the Jedi, led by Revan and his friend [[Darth Malak|Malak]], promptly joined the war against the Mandalorians.<ref name="KOTOR42" />

Not long after, Zayne Carrick, searching for the slaver [[Dace Golliard]]—and through him, Demagol, who was actually still alive—had fellow Mandalorian [[Rohlan Dyre]], also presumed dead by the Mandalorians, contact Fett.<ref name="KOTOR 48">[[Knights of the Old Republic 48|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 48]]</ref> With Carrick banking on Fett's willingness to repay his debt to Carrick and his dislike of Demagol, he and Fett planned a false strike by the Mandalorians against an asteroid mining facility near the [[Ottega system/Legends|Ithor system]]. While Carrick anonymously contacted the Republic's [[Ministry of Defense|Defense Ministry]] to tip them off to the "attack", Fett and the Mandalorians sent an unscrambled message for Golliard to intercept. Fett and his fleet arrived in the asteroid field on schedule, facing off against Admiral [[Saul Karath]] of the Republic while Golliard, expecting a battle from which he could scavenge, waited in hiding nearby. As Fett and the Mandalorians closed on Karath's fleets and Karath prepared to engage, the Mandalorians suddenly leapt to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and Golliard was left to the mercy of the Republic and Carrick. Fett contacted Carrick after the engagement to let him know the Mandalorians' debt was repaid. He commanded Dyre to keep his survival a secret, and hoped that the group would be able to kill Demagol.<ref name="KOTOR 49">[[Knights of the Old Republic 49|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 49]]</ref>

Fett would later engage Revan and Malak in the [[Battle of Jaga's Cluster]],<ref name="NEC" /> where he became infamous for killing the [[Unidentified Republic captain (Cassus Fett)|captain of a Republic flagship]]. Fett eventually became the most wanted man in known space. The Mandalorian Wars were ultimately won by the Republic, and by [[3956 BBY]] Cassus Fett was presumed dead.<ref name="KOTOR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>

===Legacy===
Fett was a member of the Mandalorians' [[Clan Fett]], and had at least [[Cassus Fett's son|one son]].<ref name="KOTORCG" /> A suit of [[Cassus Fett's Battle Armor|Fett's armor]] ultimately wound up in the possession of the merchant [[Adum Larp]] of [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], and his [[Cassus Fett's heavy blaster pistol|heavy blaster pistol]] likewise came into the keeping of [[B'ree]], a [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] merchant on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].<ref name="KOTOR" />

During the [[Mandalorian civil war (Third Galactic War)|Mandalorian Civil War]] between the [[Mandalorian Trat'ade|Mandalorian Tra'tade]] and the [[Hidden Chain]], [[Durn Wynnward]] reflected upon Fett's influence concerning the Neo-Crusaders and their rise under his leadership.<ref name="Kol's Insurrection">{{TORcite|type=codex|exp=lots|Be'manda Skira: Kol's Insurrection}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Cassus_Handbook.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Cassus Fett.]]
Cassus Fett was almost unrivaled in his knowledge of tactics and held a reputation as a master of command.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> A methodical man, he was the architect of many of Mandalore's victories in the war,<ref name="Handbook" /> and by the time of the invasion of Taris he held the position of [[Field Marshal/Legends|Field Marshal]].<ref name="Taris Special" /> He was knowledgeable in a number of languages, including [[High Galactic (language)|High Galactic]] and [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']]. Fett's influence extended throughout Mandalorian affairs, reshaping their entire culture. His contributions to Mandalorian strategy ranged even down to the mundane matters of traffic control.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Fett's notions of exerting control over the Crusaders and utilizing logistics were foreign to the Mandalorians, but they aided the Mandalorian cause greatly, turning them into a force capable of threatening the Republic's influence on the galaxy.<ref name="KOTOR 20" /> Fett also used trickery as a tactic, which fellow Mandalorian Rohlan Dyre considered to be disreputable and a mark against Mandalore the Ultimate for relying on it.<ref name="KOTOR 48" /> While Fett understood that his system was unorthodox to the Mandalorians and their nomadic way of life, he believed it would bring them ultimate victory.<ref name="KOTOR 23" /> Fett was known by his fellow Mandalorians for the ruthless tactics he used, willing to wipe out millions to preserve the greater cause of his people.<ref name="KOTOR 28" /> He would freely kill large populations for revenge, to eliminate a potential threat, or to preserve Mandalorian honor.<ref name="NEC" /><ref name="KOTOR42" /><ref name="KOTOR 28" /> Republic propaganda portrayed Fett as Mandalore the Ultimate's thug.<ref name="Interference" />

Fett considered the Mandalorians to be a new order, replacing the old order of Republic rule. He spoke of himself as a liberator of worlds, believing that the Republic's inability to defend them made it unworthy of those worlds' allegiance, and offering the path of the Neo-Crusaders to them with the promise of joining the Mandalorians in victory. Though he offered peace to those who joined the Mandalorians, he would not brook betrayal and promised death to those who feigned loyalty.<ref name="Taris Special" /> Fett did not trust his enemies easily, but once convinced of their sincerity, he was willing to put himself in their debt and repay those same debts.<ref name="KOTOR 27" /><ref name="KOTOR 28" /><ref name="KOTOR 49" />

Fett had little in the way of peers or friends. He held rivalries with other Mandalorians, such as Demagol and Pulsipher, and was glad to hear when they were reported dead.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Even Mandalore's appreciation of Demagol did not prevent Fett from aiding in his death.<ref name="KOTOR 49" /> For his part, Pulsipher did not think highly of him and was upset at the amount of resources Mandalore put in Fett's hands.<ref name="KOTOR 27" />

==Equipment==
To show his support for the color-coded system of armor he helped implement, Fett wore for a time a suit of yellow [[Neo-Crusader armor]], initially donned only by Mandalore the Ultimate's closest advisors.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> Fett also possessed at least one other suit of armor, colored purple. He wielded an adaptable heavy blaster pistol, which he was rarely seen without.<ref name="KOTOR" />

Fett could issue orders to Mandalorian [[Kandosii-type dreadnaught|''Kandosii''-type dreadnaughts]] to use [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|fission weapons]], which saw use many times in the course of the war.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> At times he led soldiers mounted on Basilisk war droids into battle, as he did at Cathar.<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Cassus Fett is one of those figures like Darth Vader that's best used sparingly—he's often present in name only, where we can see how others react to his mention.|John Jackson Miller|<ref name="JJM KOTOR 27">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor27.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #27''}}</ref>}}
[[File:Cassusfett.jpg|thumb|right|150px|[[Canderous Ordo]] wearing Fett's armor and wielding his pistol.]]
Cassus Fett was first mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', where the player can purchase his armor and blaster from two vendors; brief bits of information on Fett are provided in the flavor text for these items. There are in fact two items in the game's files labeled "Cassus Fett's Battle Armor," though only one is included in the game; the other, unused, has a different texture.

Though Fett's first actual appearance was in the comic [[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]], this was not known for some time, as the comic itself does not identify him. When [[John Jackson Miller]], author of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] comics series, was asked as to whether the Mandalorian in question was Fett, he stated that he would let the "text speak for itself" and not reveal it elsewhere.<ref name="DH boards">{{DHBoards|subdomain=www|url=Help/Boards/viewtopic.php?t=10089|thread=KNIGHTS of the OLD REPUBLIC #8 - The doctor will see you now|user=John Jackson Miller|date=2006-09-20|time=4:07 pm}} on the [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] Message Boards, Sep 20, 2006 5:34 pm and 6:06 pm</ref> Fett was first named in the series in ''[[The Taris Holofeed: Invasion Edition]]'' feature of [[Knights of the Old Republic 18|issue 18]] and first associated with a picture in the subsequent ''[[The Taris Holofeed Special Proclamation]]'' feature of [[Knights of the Old Republic 21|issue 21]], which reused a panel from 8 for its image of Fett. Miller at last made explicit that the Mandalorian in 8 was Fett in the notes for the ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook]]'' on his website.<ref name="JJM Handbook">{{Faraway|url=comics/swkotorhb1.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic Handbook #1''}}</ref>

Fett also received a profile in the ''Handbook''. The images from issue 8 were too small for inclusion, and his appearance in [[Knights of the Old Republic 23|issue 23]] was not available yet, so a red-armored Mandalorian from [[Knights of the Old Republic 20|issue 20]] was recolored gold to represent him. However, the character himself is not actually in issue 20.<ref name="JJM Handbook" />

===Inspirations===
Miller has compared Fett to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in that he feels the character is best used sparingly. Many of Fett's inclusions in the series are as a result in name only, where the reader only sees how other characters react to him.<ref name="JJM KOTOR 27" />

Miller spent some time trying to find the right tone for Fett's speech in ''The Taris Holofeed Special Proclamation'', in which the character announces that the Mandalorians are now in charge of Taris. On a vacation to [[Wikipedia:New Mexico|New Mexico]], he found his inspiration in a speech by General [[Wikipedia:Stephen W. Kearny|Stephen W. Kearny]], who made the proclamation that claimed New Mexico as part of the [[Wikipedia:United States|United States]]. Though the situation there was different from the one Miller was writing, he used it as a loose model for the terseness of Fett's announcement.<ref name="JJM KOTOR 21">{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor21.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #21''}}</ref>
